title: Multifunctional solar panel
abstract: The present invention relates to a new renewable energy solar panel and solar panel installation system which features a unique product design and configuration suitable for both horizontal roof and vertical wall installation. The vertically or horizontally mountable solar panels of the invention, and related connector channels, aim to address, at least in part, a need for appropriate installations for the harnessing of solar energy from all available building areas, including building facades.
claims:
Claims Claims: 1. A vertically or horizontally mountable solar panel comprising an outward facing side, a facade facing side, and a plurality of layers, wherein the layers include: a panel of transparent prismatic material, a first layer of photovoltaic (PV) solar cells electrically connected together in series facing outwards, a layer of backing material, LED lights integrally formed within at least one of the layers, wherein: the layers are sealed into the panel by four side-frames; the LED lights are powered by electricity generated by the PV solar cells. 2. The solar panel of claim 1 , where the backing material is a reflective or transparent material, wherein a reflective side is facing outwards. 3. The solar panel of claim 1 wherein the LED lights are integrally formed within a further layer. 4. The solar panel of claim 1 , wherein the LED lights are integrally formed within a further layer of a corrugated material comprising at least two grooves with LED lights positioned in said grooves. 5. The solar panel of claim 1 , wherein the transparent prismatic material, the backing material and/or the corrugated material is polycarbonate or acrylic. 6. The solar panel of claim 1 , wherein the prisms on the transparent prismatic layer face towards the facade and the smooth surface of the prismatic material is facing outwards. 7. The solar panel of claim 1 , further comprising a second layer of PV solar cells electronically connected together in series positioned between the first layer of PV solar cells and the layer of backing material. 8. The solar panel of claim 1 , wherein at least two side frames are adapted for use with a connector channel capable of reversibly connecting at least one solar panel to another solar panel. 9. A connector channel for reversibly connecting at least two solar panels having side-frames, comprising: 

 an electrical wiring compartment that extends the length of the connector channel, wherein the electrical wiring compartment is flanked either side by holder channels that accept a side frame from each solar panel and the electrical wiring compartment is adapted to accept electrical wiring for the solar cells that exits the solar panels via the side frames; a facade attachment point; and a removable cover for the electrical wiring compartment, wherein the connector channel has a length equal to or substantially equal to the length of at least one of the side frames being accepted by the holder channels. 10. A connector channel for reversibly connecting at least two solar panels as defined in claim 1 , comprising: an electrical wiring compartment that extends the length of the connector channel, wherein the electrical wiring compartment is flanked either side by holder channels that accept a side frame from each solar panel and the electrical wiring compartment is adapted to accept the electrical wiring for the LED lights and/or the PV solar cells that exit the solar panels via the side frames of the solar panels; a facade attachment point; and a removable cover for the electrical wiring compartment, wherein the connector channel has a length substantially equal to the length of at least one of the side frames being accepted by the holder channels. 11. The connector channel of claim 9 or claim 10, wherein the electrical wiring compartment is at least water-resistant when sealed by the removable cover. 12. The connector channel of claim 9 or claim 10, wherein the removable cover is a clip-on cover. 13. The connector channel of claim 9 or claim 10, wherein the electrical wiring compartment further comprises the electrical wiring connection box. 14. The connector channel of claim 13, wherein the electrical wiring compartment comprises at least one component selected from the group consisting of an AC/DC inverter, a battery, an LED light, a light sensor and WiFi device. The connector channel of claim 9 or claim 10, wherein the attachment point allows the panel to be affixed to any vertical or horizontal surface area by way of an attachment bolt and bolt hole centrally housed in the electrical wiring compartment.
